title: Bank of England Weekly Report 25 March 2026
summary: The Bank of England published its weekly balance sheet data as of 25 March 2026, showing reserves balances increased to £647.6 billion from £637.0 billion the prior week. Short-term repo operations rose to £98.1 billion, while the Loan to Asset Purchase Facility remained unchanged at £553.2 billion.
key_points: Reserves balances: £647,608mn (25 March) versus £636,991mn (18 March), an increase of £10,617mn
Short-term repo operations: £98,071mn, up from £96,967mn the prior week
Loan to Asset Purchase Facility: £553,158mn, unchanged week-over-week
Indexed long-term repo: £72,451mn, down slightly from £72,631mn
Term Funding Scheme with additional SME incentives: £41,894mn, unchanged
Foreign currency reserve assets: £18,989mn, up from £18,644mn
why_it_matters: This is mechanical balance sheet data rather than policy content. The weekly report provides transparency on the Bank of England's operational position and liquidity-providing facilities. Changes in reserves balances and repo operations can signal near-term liquidity conditions in the sterling money market, though this is a historical data release with no forward guidance.
market_structure_context: The report details the Bank's use of various monetary operations tools including short-term repo, indexed long-term repo, and the Term Funding Scheme. Rising reserves balances indicate increased liquidity in the banking system. The unchanged Loan to Asset Purchase Facility (£553.2bn) shows no change to QE holdings. Foreign currency operations (reserve assets and public securities issued) are minimal relative to sterling operations.
notes: Source is a factual balance sheet data release with no policy commentary or forward guidance. All numbers directly extracted from the weekly report table. The content word count (242 words) is thin but contains valid numerical data. No policy signals present in this mechanical release.
